stem: Line h is defined by (1/4)x + (1/9)y − 30 = 0. Line j is perpendicular to line h in the xy-plane. What is the slope of line j?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. Solving (1/4)x + (1/9)y − 30 = 0 for y: (1/9)y = −(1/4)x + 30, so y = −(9/4)x + 270. The slope of line h is −9/4. The slope of a perpendicular line is the negative reciprocal: 4/9.

Choice B is the reciprocal without sign change. Choice C uses the wrong sign. Choice D is the slope of h itself.
